content.problemStatement:
Chef is collecting special ingredients in a cave. The cave has NNN chambers and N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 passageways forming a tree. In other words, it is possible to move from one chamber to any other chamber using the passageways. Each passageway is of equal length and can be traversed in both directions.

At 000-th second, Chef is at chamber AjA_jAj‚Äã. At the start of each second, he can choose to either stay in the current chamber or move along a passageway to another chamber in 111 second. To collect more ingredients, he wants to visit as many distinct chambers as possible before leaving the cave at chamber BjB_jBj‚Äã.

There is a ghost that copies Chef's movement with a delay of KjK_jKj‚Äã seconds. This means that:


Before KjK_jKj‚Äã-th second, the ghost is not in the cave yet.
At KjK_jKj‚Äã-th second, the ghost appears in chamber AjA_jAj‚Äã.
At ttt-th second (t‚â•Kjt \ge K_jt‚â•Kj‚Äã), the ghost will be in the same place that Chef was at (t‚àíKj)(t - K_j)(t‚àíKj‚Äã)-th second.
The ghost will capture the Chef if, at some moment of time, both of them are in the same chamber or using the same passageway, including the moment when Chef is about to leave at chamber BjB_jBj‚Äã.

For a given start chamber AjA_jAj‚Äã, end chamber BjB_jBj‚Äã and ghost movement delay KjK_jKj‚Äã, help Chef determine the maximum number of distinct chambers he can visit. You have to answer QQQ such queries for the same cave.


Input Format


The first line contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ, representing the number of chambers and queries respectively.




Each of the next N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 lines contains two space-separated integers UiU_iUi‚Äã and ViV_iVi‚Äã, representing the two endpoints of a passageway.




Each of the next qqq lines contains three space-separated integers AjA_jAj‚Äã, BjB_jBj‚Äã and KjK_jKj‚Äã, representing the start chamber, end chamber and ghost's movement delay for a query respectively.





Output Format
For each query, output on a single line the maximum number of distinct chambers that Chef can visit.
constraints: The chambers and passageways form a tree.
**Subtask 1** (10 points): Each chamber is connected by at most two passageways.
